formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Newton's Second Law of Motion for a Particle Moving in a Plane",
    "formula": "F = m(a_x, a_y)",
    "variables": "F: net force acting on the particle, m: mass of the particle, a_x, a_y: x and y components of acceleration",
    "units": "F: N, m: kg, a_x, a_y: m/s^2",
    "notes": "This law states that the net force acting on a particle is equal to the mass of the particle multiplied by its acceleration."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Net Acceleration",
    "formula": "a^2 = a_x^2 + a_y^2",
    "variables": "a: magnitude of net acceleration, a_x, a_y: x and y components of acceleration",
    "units": "a: m/s^2, a_x, a_y: m/s^2",
    "notes": "This expression gives the magnitude of the net acceleration as the square root of the sum of the squares of its x and y components."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Net Displacement",
    "formula": "r^2 = x^2 + y^2",
    "variables": "r: magnitude of net displacement, x, y: x and y components of displacement",
    "units": "r: m, x, y: m",
    "notes": "This expression gives the magnitude of the net displacement as the square root of the sum of the squares of its x and y components."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Net Velocity",
    "formula": "v^2 = v_x^2 + v_y^2",
    "variables": "v: magnitude of net velocity, v_x, v_y: x and y components of velocity",
    "units": "v: m/s, v_x, v_y: m/s",
    "notes": "This expression gives the magnitude of the net velocity as the square root of the sum of the squares of its x and y components."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Tangential and Normal Components of Acceleration",
    "formula": "a_t = a \* cos(θ), a_n = a \* sin(θ)",
    "variables": "a_t: tangential component of acceleration, a_n: normal component of acceleration, a: magnitude of net acceleration, θ: angle between the direction of motion and the x-axis",
    "units": "a_t, a_n: m/s^2, a: m/s^2, θ: rad",
    "notes": "These expressions give the tangential and normal components of acceleration in terms of the magnitude of the net acceleration and the angle between the direction of motion and the x-axis."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Tangential and Normal Components of Velocity",
    "formula": "v_t = v \* cos(θ), v_n = v \* sin(θ)",
    "variables": "v_t: tangential component of velocity, v_n: normal component of velocity, v: magnitude of net velocity, θ: angle between the direction of motion and the x-axis",
    "units": "v_t, v_n: m/s, v: m/s, θ: rad",
    "notes": "These expressions give the tangential and normal components of velocity in terms of the magnitude of the net velocity and the angle between the direction of motion and the x-axis."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Angular Velocity",
    "formula": "ω = Δθ / Δt",
    "variables": "ω: angular velocity, Δθ: change in angle, Δt: time taken to produce the change in angle",
    "units": "ω: rad/s, Δθ: rad, Δt: s",
    "notes": "This expression gives the angular velocity in terms of the change in angle and the time taken to produce the change."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Angular Displacement",
    "formula": "θ = ωt + θ_0",
    "variables": "θ: angular displacement, ω: angular velocity, t: time, θ_0: initial angular displacement",
    "units": "θ: rad, ω: rad/s, t: s, θ_0: rad",
    "notes": "This expression gives the angular displacement in terms of the angular velocity, time, and initial angular displacement."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Angular Velocity in Terms of Linear Velocity",
    "formula": "ω = v / r",
    "variables": "ω: angular velocity, v: linear velocity, r: radius of the circular path",
    "units": "ω: rad/s, v: m/s, r: m",
    "notes": "This expression gives the angular velocity in terms of the linear velocity and the radius of the circular path."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Linear Velocity in Terms of Angular Velocity",
    "formula": "v = rω",
    "variables": "v: linear velocity, r: radius of the circular path, ω: angular velocity",
    "units": "v: m/s, r: m, ω: rad/s",
    "notes": "This expression gives the linear velocity in terms of the angular velocity and the radius of the circular path."
  },
  {
    "name": "Expression for Centripetal Acceleration",
    "formula": "a_c = v^2 / r",
    "variables": "a_c: centripetal acceleration, v: linear velocity, r: radius
